data.title: DBS Chief Economist: Japan, Singapore, and Australia May Raise Interest Rates
data.description: DBS Bank's Chief Economist suggests that Japan, Singapore, and Australia appear likely to increase interest rates. According to Jin10, the economist also noted that the Bank of Korea could adopt a proactive approach similar to the Reserve Bank of Australia.

data.title: DBS Bank Chief Economist: Inflation Impact from Middle East Conflict Seems Certain
data.description: According to Jin10, the chief economist at DBS Bank has stated that the inflationary impact of the Middle East conflict appears to be inevitable. While the crisis in the Middle East is not expected to significantly harm economic growth in Asia, inflation remains a more pressing concern.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Continues Rally Amid Anticipated Trump Speech
data.description:
U.S. stock markets extended gains from the previous day as U.S. President Donald Trump is set to deliver a nationwide address. According to RTHK, White House officials indicated that Trump will reiterate plans to conclude the conflict with Iran within two to three weeks. However, Iran has denied requesting a ceasefire from the United States.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose over 460 points at its peak, surpassing 46,800 points, and closed at 46,565 points, up 224 points or approximately 0.5%, marking a three-day consecutive rise and reaching a two-week high. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq indices also recorded gains for the second consecutive day, closing at one-week highs. The S&P 500 closed at 6,575 points, up 46 points or over 0.7%, while the Nasdaq closed at 21,840 points, up 250 points or about 1.2%.

Technology stocks performed well, with notable increases in chip and memory chip stocks. Intel, Micron, SanDisk, and Western Digital saw gains of nearly 9% to 10%, while Nvidia, Apple, Amazon, and Meta rose by about 1% or more. Alphabet and Tesla increased by approximately 3%.

Additionally, the Wall Street Journal reported that the Trump administration is preparing to adjust tariffs on steel and aluminum. The plan reportedly includes maintaining a 50% tariff on bulk steel and aluminum products and imposing a 25% tariff on finished goods made from imported steel and aluminum, with an announcement expected as early as this week. In after-hours trading, shares of Alcoa and Century Aluminum fell by over 1%.

data.title: Sentora Unaffected by Drift Protocol Hack
data.description: Sentora has announced that its vaults and investment strategies remain secure following the recent hack on Drift Protocol. According to NS3.AI, Sentora is actively monitoring the situation to ensure continued protection and stability for its operations.

data.title: U.S. Intelligence Agencies Assess Iran's Stance on Negotiations
data.description: Multiple U.S. intelligence agencies have concluded that Iran is not prepared to engage in negotiations to end the ongoing conflict. According to NS3.AI, the assessment indicates that Iran harbors doubts about U.S. President Donald Trump's expressed willingness to negotiate.

data.title: The U.S. Treasury Department has released the first draft of implementing rules for the GENIUS Act, seeking public comment.
data.description: The U.S. Treasury Department has released its first proposed rulemaking document (NPRM) for the GENIUS Act, a stablecoin regulatory bill, marking the bill's entry into the implementation phase. This proposal focuses on the regulatory path for "small stablecoin issuers," proposing criteria to assess whether state-level regulatory systems are "substantially equivalent" to the federal framework. Under the bill, institutions with issuances below $10 billion can choose to be regulated by states, provided their regulatory systems comply with federal standards. The Treasury Department stated that the proposal aims to clarify the regulatory boundaries between states and the federal government and provide a clearer compliance path for the stablecoin industry. The public will have 60 days to submit comments on the draft rule. Meanwhile, other federal regulatory agencies, including the FDIC and OCC, are also developing related supporting rules, and the overall stablecoin regulatory framework is gradually taking shape.

data.title: Bonk.fun's April Fools' Prank Sparks Political Satire Debate
data.description: Bonk.fun recently used April Fools' Day to release a satirical post about a fictional 'Trench Guard' feature, suggesting that users in Israel would be restricted from trading. According to NS3.AI, the post was intended as political satire rather than an actual compliance update. This move has sparked discussions about the boundaries of humor and satire in the context of sensitive geopolitical issues.

data.title: The Crypto Political Action Committee appoints a Tether executive as chairperson, escalating the stablecoin controversy.
data.description: As divisions intensify in the US over stablecoin policy, the crypto super political action committee, Fellowship PAC, announced the appointment of Jesse Spiro, a Tether US executive, as chair to lead a political campaign supporting pro-crypto candidates. This appointment comes as the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act stalls in the Senate. The current point of contention is whether platforms should be allowed to offer yields or incentives for stablecoin holdings, a key obstacle preventing the bill from reaching a consensus. Recently, some crypto companies, including Coinbase, have expressed reservations about the latest version of the bill, while stablecoin issuer Circle is facing market pressure due to policy uncertainty. Analysts point out that with the midterm elections approaching, the crypto industry is intensifying its political lobbying efforts, attempting to secure a more favorable policy environment in terms of regulation.

data.title: US intelligence agencies say Iran currently has no intention of engaging in substantive negotiations and believes Trump lacks sincerity.
data.description: According to The New York Times, U.S. officials revealed that multiple U.S. intelligence agencies recently assessed that the Iranian government is currently unwilling to engage in substantive negotiations to end the U.S.-Israel war. Officials stated that the assessment suggests the Iranian government believes it is in a dominant position during the war and is not obligated to comply with U.S. diplomatic demands. They said that although Iran is willing to maintain open communication channels, the country distrusts the United States and believes President Trump is not genuinely committed to negotiations. In the past year, Trump ordered attacks on Iran twice during negotiations on its nuclear program. These assessments are consistent with recent statements from Iranian officials. U.S. and Iranian officials stated that the two countries are exchanging information through intermediary countries and perhaps directly, but have not negotiated the conditions for a ceasefire or an end to the war.

data.title: EDX, backed by Charles Schwab, Citadel, and Fidelity, has applied for a U.S. trust banking license.
data.description: EDX Markets, a crypto trading platform backed by Charles Schwab, Citadel Securities, and Fidelity, has applied to the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) for a National Trust Bank license. This license will allow EDX to provide services such as digital asset custody, asset management, and transaction settlement, but will not include traditional banking services such as deposit-taking or lending. EDX stated that obtaining this license will help it better serve institutional clients and gain a competitive edge in the institutionalization wave of the crypto industry. Recently, several other crypto companies, including Ripple, Circle, BitGo, Paxos, and Fidelity Digital Assets, have also applied for similar licenses, indicating that the industry is rapidly moving towards a regulated financial system.

data.title: Volatility Shares Expands ETF Offerings with New Crypto Funds
data.description: Volatility Shares has introduced six new exchange-traded funds (ETFs) on Wednesday, featuring 2x leveraged funds for Cardano, Stellar, and Chainlink. According to NS3.AI, the launch also includes standard futures funds linked to these three cryptocurrencies. This expansion enhances the firm's portfolio of crypto exchange-traded products available in the U.S.

data.title: Ukraine's Attacks on Russian Oil Facilities Impact Export Capacity
data.description: Ukraine has intensified its attacks on Russian oil facilities over the past week, aiming to hinder Moscow's ability to capitalize on potential international oil price increases following U.S. and Israeli actions against Iran. According to Ming Pao, at least two oil export ports facing the Baltic Sea and one refinery in Russia have been damaged and burning for several days due to Ukrainian military strikes. These attacks have temporarily reduced Russia's oil export capacity by at least 40%.

data.title: Hong Kong Real Estate Stocks Face Uncertainty Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Despite better-than-expected performance by Hong Kong real estate stocks, the sector faces macroeconomic uncertainties due to the Middle East conflict, according to Ming Pao. Major financial institution JP Morgan has expressed concerns about potential interest rate hikes and a mild economic recession. The bank currently anticipates that interest rates will remain unchanged until the first quarter of 2027.

data.title: Global Stock Markets Rebound as Trump Hints at Possible Troop Withdrawal from Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has indicated the possibility of withdrawing troops from Iran within the next two to three weeks, sparking optimism about the potential end of the conflict. According to Ming Pao, this development led to a rebound in global stock markets, with Asian markets experiencing their largest surge in nearly a year. Japanese, South Korean, and Taiwanese stocks led the gains, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose by 2%, reclaiming the 25,000-point level. The technology index saw its largest increase in over three weeks, and pharmaceutical and automotive stocks were also in demand. However, some investors took the opportunity to sell off their holdings.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Rebound as Middle East Tensions Ease
data.description: Hong Kong's stock market experienced a rebound following the easing of tensions in the Middle East, aligning with the recovery seen in global markets. According to Ming Pao, this positive start marks the beginning of the second quarter. However, the extent of the market's recovery varies, with the pharmaceutical and biotechnology sectors notably attracting investment and showing improvement. Despite the more optimistic atmosphere, uncertainties remain present.

data.title: Bitcoin Transfer from Wintermute to Anchorage Digital
data.description: A significant transfer of Bitcoin has been reported. According to ChainCatcher, at 03:55, a total of 507.92 BTC was moved from Wintermute to Anchorage Digital.

data.title: CFTC Settles Fraud Case Against Former FTX Executive Nishad Singh
data.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has concluded its fraud case against Nishad Singh, the former engineering chief at FTX. According to NS3.AI, Singh has been ordered to return $3.7 million in illegal profits as part of the settlement. Additionally, Singh is prohibited from trading for a period of five years. The CFTC noted that it did not pursue a monetary penalty or restitution due to Singh's cooperation during the investigation.

data.title: IRGC Threatens Major US Companies in Middle East as Legitimate Targets
data.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has issued a warning that major U.S. companies operating in the Middle East will be considered legitimate targets starting April 1. According to NS3.AI, the IRGC specifically named firms such as Google, JPMorgan Chase, and Tesla. These companies are involved in sectors like cloud services, payments, and corporate treasury, which are integral to various parts of the digital asset industry.

data.title: Drift Faces $285 Million Crypto Exploit
data.description: Drift experienced a significant security breach on Wednesday, resulting in the theft of approximately $285 million in cryptocurrency. According to NS3.AI, the incident was flagged by several firms, including PeckShield. Blockchain data revealed that some of the stolen assets were converted into USDC.

data.title: Australia Integrates Crypto Service Providers into Financial Services Framework
data.description: Australia has officially incorporated crypto service providers into its financial services regulatory framework. According to NS3.AI, this marks the first time that such providers are included within the country's formal financial services regime. This regulatory shift represents a significant change for the crypto industry in Australia.
